_raw_content: Al-Sukūn. The resting point encoded in your nervous system before the world wrote its noise over it. You were not born braced. You were not born scanning. There was a frequency beneath the first wound — and it never left. It only got buried under the velocity of surviving. Healing is not the addition of new knowledge. It is the subtraction of everything that convinced you stillness was unsafe. When the body finally returns to its zero point, it does not feel like peace. It feels like remembering.
subtitle: "The Stillness Before All Movement"
slides: Before the wound chose your reaction, there was a stillness inside you that knew exactly what to do.
Your nervous system does not need more information. It needs permission to stop.
You were not born dysregulated. You were taught to leave the center. The center never left you.
The body remembers a frequency it lived in before language, before fear, before the first betrayal taught it to brace.
Healing is not adding. It is the return to what was there before the noise began.
Stillness is not the absence of pain. It is the presence of something older than pain.
When the system finally rests, it does not feel like peace. It feels like remembering.
caption:
You have been solving for peace as though it were something to build.

It is not.

It is something to uncover.

Beneath every pattern of bracing, every loop of hypervigilance, every story that says you must earn your safety — there is a stillness that predates all of it.

Your nervous system knew rest before it knew threat. It knew openness before it learned to wall. The dysregulation was never yours. It was installed. And the body has been waiting — not for a technique, not for a breakthrough — but for the moment you stop long enough to feel what was already there.

Sukūn is not collapse. It is not numbing. It is the original state your system has been trying to return to since the very first time it was pulled away.

You do not need to become regulated. You need to stop rehearsing the reasons you cannot be.
